message:
test(codec/proptest): strengthen compressed-flag sentinel to use non-empty varbincode body

The compressed-flag sentinel test `structured_header_helper_uses_real_codec_compression_bit`
was using an empty-body Pdu::Ping { } frame as the reference fixture. For
an empty body, flipping the compressed flag on the length word is almost
a no-op: zstd-decoding zero bytes yields zero bytes, and the downstream
varbincode deserializer happens to accept the empty byte slice for Ping.
That made the test's "compressed-flagged frame should hit zstd decode and
fail" assertion weakly-grounded — it could pass even if the decoder never
routed through zstd at all.

Replace the fixture with an encoded Pdu::ErrorResponse whose `reason`
field is the non-empty sentinel string "compressed-flag sentinel":

  let expected = Pdu::ErrorResponse(ErrorResponse {
      reason: "compressed-flag sentinel".to_string(),
  });
  let mut canonical = Vec::new();
  expected
      .encode_with_mode(&mut canonical, 77, CompressionMode::Never)
      .expect("encode canonical uncompressed frame");
  let (serial, ident, body) = split_single_frame(canonical.as_slice());

Then feed that canonical (serial, ident, body) triple back through the
`structured_header_frame` helper — once uncompressed (must round-trip to
the same ErrorResponse) and once with the compressed flag flipped
(raw-varbincode bytes are NOT valid zstd, so the decoder must surface
a typed error).

Helper addition: `split_single_frame(frame)` parses a canonical encoded
frame back into (serial, ident, body) by reading the three leb128 header
fields and returning the remaining bytes as the body. Used only by the
sentinel test to pull the canonical components out of a
Pdu::encode_with_mode output — no proptest coupling.

Import cleanup: drop `Ping`, drop `PING_IDENT = 1` const, add
`CompressionMode` and `ErrorResponse` imports. Keep `COMPRESSED_MASK`
const since the `structured_header_frame` helper still consumes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
